event: Miracle of the Archangel Michael of Flor and Laurus
symbols: archangel Michael with wings — the heavenly protector and commander of God's armies
horses of many colors — the herds entrusted to the protection of the saints
saints standing on either side — Florus and Laurus as patrons of horses and livestock
golden background — the divine realm in which the miracle takes place
description: A Russian icon on a golden background. At the top center, the Archangel Michael stands with large dark wings spread wide, holding a staff. Flanking him are two standing saints in military dress. Below them, the composition is filled with horses of various colors — white, black, brown, red, and gray — along with riders and herdsmen. The horses are arranged in rows covering most of the panel's lower two-thirds. At the bottom, additional animals including cattle graze. The overall composition is densely packed and highly stylized in traditional Russian icon style.
theological_description: This icon depicts the popular Russian Orthodox tradition of Saints Florus and Laurus, twin brothers martyred in the second century, who became patron saints of horses. The Archangel Michael presides over them as commander of the heavenly hosts (REV.12.7). Though the saints are post-biblical, the icon's theology draws on scriptural themes: God's care for all creatures (PSA.36.6: 'You save humans and animals alike, O LORD'), the ministry of angels on behalf of the faithful (HEB.1.14), and the vision of the heavenly hosts on horseback (REV.19.14). The multiple colored horses recall the vision of Zechariah 6:1-7, where chariots with red, black, white, and dappled horses patrol the earth. Michael's central position affirms Daniel 12:1: 'At that time Michael, the great prince who protects your people, will arise.'

scripture_refs.reason: Michael the archangel, commander of the heavenly hosts — his central role in the icon

Chariots with horses of different colors patrolling the earth — the multicolored horses

Michael the great prince who protects God's people
